commands.review_summary:
The PR broadens and fixes plugin metadata snapshot memoization, threads metadata snapshots and discovery through provider, activation, auto-enable, and bundled-channel paths, and adds focused regression coverage for cold-start plugin work.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; this is a performance PR. The author provides live cold-start measurements and focused regression tests, and source review confirms the changed hot paths are the plugin metadata/provider startup paths described.
commands.review_followup: This is a maintainer-labeled PR with no current blocking code finding; the remaining action is maintainer validation and merge judgment, not an automated repair.

target.title: perf(plugins): cut plugin-system thrash from TUI cold start
target.body:
## Summary

Cuts plugin-system work during TUI cold start. Freeze drops from ~32s to ~13–16s; residual cost is dominated by two single-plugin module loads (Codex 2.76s, Anthropic 746ms) that this PR doesn't touch.

The work is four core fixes, ranked by single-fix impact:

### 1. Cache-busting — snapshot memo store/lookup keys now match (~7s)

Previously the memo computed its lookup key from the persisted index and stored entries under the derived index. Those indexes always differ in volatile fields (`generatedAtMs`, `refreshReason`), so the keys never agreed and every lookup missed. **Hit rate goes from 24% → 98%.** Single biggest commit in the PR.

### 2. Plumbing — `PluginMetadataSnapshot` routed through resolve paths (~2–4s)

`resolvePluginProviders`, `isPluginProvidersLoadInFlight`, `resolveOwningPluginIdsForProvider`, and `resolveExternalAuthProfilesWithPlugins` all dispatch to helpers that have a manifestRegistry fast path. They now thread the snapshot through so the fast path actually fires instead of every helper rebuilding the manifest registry from disk. `loadPluginManifestRegistryForInstalledIndex` fires drop 685 → ~10.

A second plumb threads `discovery?: PluginDiscoveryResult` through `applyPluginAutoEnable` → `detectPluginAutoEnableCandidates` → `resolvePluginAutoEnableReadiness` → `collectConfiguredChannelIds` → the bundled-channel leaf helpers, so a caller's discovery short-circuits the cascade.

Only delivers value because (1) made the snapshot memo actually work.

### 3. LRU capacity (supporting)

Memo was single-slot and thrashed when two stable input shapes alternated. Capacity-8 LRU keeps both. Necessary precondition for the cache-busting fix to land cleanly — without LRU, the memo evicts itself.

### 4. Eligibility predicate broadened (supporting)

The memo previously only stored derived snapshots whose diagnostics were *exclusively* `persisted-registry-stale-policy`, which discarded ~98% of cold-start derives. Now stores every non-empty derived snapshot. To keep the invalidation surface honest, the cached entry's `watchedFiles` set is widened for derived snapshots to include the derived index's plugin manifest paths; the next lookup re-stats them and re-derives on any change.

## Verification

- `pnpm tsgo --noEmit --project tsconfig.core.json` — clean
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/plugin-metadata-snapshot.memo.test.ts src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.test.ts src/plugins/providers.test.ts` — 93/93 pass (includes a new regression for derived-only manifest edits)
- `pnpm build && pnpm openclaw tui` from a cold process — freeze 32s → 13–16s, snapshot memo hit rate 24% → 98%, per-plugin `package.json` reads ~914 → ~370

Not run: full `pnpm test` / `pnpm check`, sibling-process plugin-install interleaving.

## Notes

- Three `wip(perf-trace):` commits are preserved in history so the audit instrumentation is easy to cherry-pick for future startup work. The chore() commit at the tip reverts their effects, so net diff against main contains zero `perfMark`/`perfSpan` calls.
- An `installRecords` threading optimization was pulled into [`dallin-perf-install-records-threading`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/tree/dallin-perf-install-records-threading) for a follow-on PR — it cut ~264 sync disk reads but only ~140ms of freeze, so not worth bundling here.
